title: Canary Routing with VirtualService
description:
## Canary Routing with VirtualService

A service named `bookinfo` runs in the `istio-lab` namespace with two `DestinationRule` subsets already defined:
- `v1` — targets pods with label `version: v1`
- `v2` — targets pods with label `version: v2`

**Your task:**

Create a `VirtualService` named `bookinfo` in the `istio-lab` namespace that routes **80%** of traffic to subset `v1` and **20%** to subset `v2`.

The VirtualService must target the host `bookinfo` and apply to all traffic (no gateway field needed).

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get virtualservice bookinfo -n istio-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.http[0].route[*].weight}'
```

hints.title: VirtualService weighted routing structure
hints.body: Use the http[].route[].weight field. Weights must sum to 100. Reference the DestinationRule subsets by name in the destination.subset field.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: bookinfo
  namespace: istio-lab
spec:
  hosts:
  - bookinfo
  http:
  - route:
    - destination:
        host: bookinfo
        subset: v1
      weight: 80
    - destination:
        host: bookinfo
        subset: v2
      weight: 20
EOF

setup_commands.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: bookinfo
  namespace: istio-lab
spec:
  host: bookinfo
  subsets:
  - name: v1
    labels:
      version: v1
  - name: v2
    labels:
      version: v2
EOF

validation.description: Check that the VirtualService has the correct 80/20 weight split

commands.command: kubectl get virtualservice bookinfo -n istio-lab -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
